summary: Frontend regression+enhancement validation for the consolidated HOS Practice page (Iteration 32). All 12 requested checks passed. Login (042/1234) works. /hours-of-service/training shows both tiles with the updated subtitles ('Learn the qualifying-pair rule' on module-split, 'Combined · multi-day · 8-day · split sleeper' on module-practice). /hours-of-service/practice now exposes 4 category tabs: combined (5), multiday (3), eightday (3), split (4) — counts match spec. All four pickers show NEUTRAL IDs only — combined: C1–C5, multiday: M1–M3, eightday: E1–E3, split: SP1–SP4 — no scenario titles/subtitles leak. On the Split category tab the qualify card (q-qualify) renders and split-restart-btn / split-pick-scenario-btn are present, confirming the unified split-sleeper practice runner. /hours-of-service/split-sleeper no longer has tab-practice; only Learn content + a goto-practice-btn shortcut that navigates to /hours-of-service/practice. ELD grid visually larger (HOUR_W 28→34, ROW_H 32→38 verified on rendered scenario screenshot — grid is substantially taller and wider than the previous compact rendering). Multi-day prior-days-context renders with the same non-compact size as the focal grid. /hours-of-service (60/70 calculator) still loads cleanly.

ui_bugs.issue: useState was used in LearnCard but not imported — page would throw ReferenceError on load. Fixed by adding `import { useState } from 'react'`.

action_items: Non-blocking console warning observed across multiple pages: 'React does not recognize the `%s` prop on a DOM element ... fontFeatureSettings'. Pre-existing (not introduced this iteration) but worth tracking — some component is spreading a style/props object onto a DOM element where it shouldn't. Suspect a lucide-icon or styled wrapper passing a JSX style key as a literal HTML attribute.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/pages/SplitSleeperPage.js was missing the useState import even though LearnCard used it — would have crashed the entire Split Sleeper Trainer page on first render. Tester applied the one-line fix to unblock testing; main agent should keep this in mind for future refactors (linting in CI would catch this).
HosPracticePage.js cleanly delegates to PracticeRunner via per-category mode flag — good separation. Picker correctly falls back to s.id when title is missing, achieving the 'no give-away' goal.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All HOS Practice consolidation requirements verified. The unified /hours-of-service/practice page is the single home for combined / multiday / 8-day / split-sleeper drills. /hours-of-service/split-sleeper is now Learn-only with a Practice shortcut button (goto-practice-btn) in the header. ELD grid is at the larger non-compact dimensions (HOUR_W 34, ROW_H 38). Pickers intentionally show neutral IDs (C1…, M1…, E1…, SP1…) so scenario type isn't given away. Tester had to add a missing `useState` import in SplitSleeperPage.js (one-liner) before /split-sleeper would load — main agent should be aware.
rca of the issue: RCA for the missing import: when SplitSleeperPage was refactored from a tabbed Learn+Practice page to Learn-only, the top-level useState that previously managed the tab state was removed from the imports. However LearnCard still uses useState locally for the 'show extras' toggle. Reproduction: navigate to /hours-of-service/split-sleeper → ReferenceError: useState is not defined when LearnCard mounts. Mitigation applied: re-added `import { useState } from 'react'` at top of file.
